pattern: Since every array element is used in exactly one piece, mapping each piece by its first value lets you reconstruct the target purely by lookahead, without ever trying piece orderings yourself.
hints: Notice that if a piece can appear in the answer at all, its first element tells you unambiguously where it must start -- you never have to guess which piece goes where.

Index the pieces by their first element in a lookup table, then walk through the target array from left to right, and whenever a new piece must start, fetch it directly by the value you're currently standing on.

After finding the right piece by its first value, check that its remaining elements match the target one by one in place -- if a lookup ever fails, or a value inside the piece doesn't line up, the array can't be formed.

edge_inputs.description: piece internal order reversed relative to the array -- reordering within a piece is not allowed
piece contains a value not present in the array at all
same value set as the array but grouped across non-contiguous positions
maximum array length (100), split into several large shuffled pieces
minimum and maximum allowed values (1 and 100)
single piece already equal to the whole target array

stress.description: build a random target array, cut it into contiguous pieces of random size, then shuffle the piece order
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    values = rng.sample(range(1, 101), n)
    idx = 0
    pieces = []
    while idx < n:
        remaining = n - idx
        take = rng.randint(1, remaining)
        pieces.append(values[idx:idx + take])
        idx += take
    rng.shuffle(pieces)
    return [values, pieces]
stress.note: pieces are always a contiguous partition of the array, so the answer is always true; catches performance regressions on the max length
